events.title: Weather tracker: Heavy rain triggers flooding and landslides in parts of China
events.summary:
Twelve dead as southern and central areas also suffer travel disruption, electricity outages and evacuations

Heavy, prolonged rainfall in southern and central China has resulted in weather warnings for flash flooding, landslides and waterlogging. The slow-moving band of rainfall spanned 620 miles (1,000km) and travelled eastwards across the regions on Tuesday. It was formed from the convergence of multiple bands of rain originating from the Bay of Bengal, South China Sea and Pacific Ocean.

Given the accompanying low wind speeds, high daily and hourly rainfall totals have been recorded, with as much as 75mm locally in Hunan, 85mm in Anhui in a 24-hour period, and 95mm on the island of Hainan. Twelve people have died because of the flooding and hundreds of residents have been evacuated by emergency services. There were major travel disruptions, electricity outages, as well as school and business closures. Social media posts showed submerged cars and people fishing along flooded streets.

events.title: Former F1 world champion Alain Prost reportedly injured in robbery at Swiss home
events.summary:
• Police investigating an alleged home invasion

  • 71-year-old sustained a head injury, says reports

Swiss police are investigating an alleged robbery with reports that the four-times Formula 1 world champion Alain Prost was injured during a home invasion. The Swiss tabloid Blick reported late on Friday that the 71-year-old Frenchman sustained a head injury from intruders who forced his son to open a safe during the incident on Tuesday morning.

“The perpetrators entered the residence while the occupants were present, threatened them, and forced one family member to open a safe before fleeing with the stolen goods,” the public prosecutor’s office said. “Despite the extensive search operation launched, the perpetrators have not yet been apprehended.”

Continue reading...

events.title: UK needs ‘national consensus’ over rejoining EU, David Miliband says
events.summary:
Ex-foreign secretary urges reset at ‘higher dosage’ after officials revealed to have pitched single market for goods

Britain needs a “national consensus” about rejoining the European Union, David Miliband has said, in response to revelations that the UK government pitched the creation of a single market for goods with the EU to the bloc.

The former foreign secretary, who is now president of the International Rescue Committee, said he thought the UK needed a reset of its relations with the EU at “a much higher dosage” than the government was planning.

Continue reading...

events.title: Pope decries ‘unscrupulous’ polluters at deadly, mafia-linked dumping ground
events.summary:
Pontiff visits ‘Land of Fires’ where dumping, burial and burning of waste has caused rise in cancer and other illnesses

Pope Leo has met Italian families whose loved ones have died or have cancer as a result of illegal toxic dumping linked to a multi-billion-euro criminal racket run by the mafia.

Leo’s visit to the Terra dei Fuochi, or Land of Fires, near Naples, came on the eve of the 11th anniversary of Pope Francis’ big ecological encyclical, Laudato Si (Praised Be), and indicates Leo’s commitment to carry on his predecessor’s environmental agenda.

Continue reading...

events.title: UK pitched single market for goods with EU in pursuit of deeper trade ties
events.summary:
Exclusive: Top British official presented idea in Brussels but sources say it was rebuffed

The UK government pitched the creation of a single market for goods with the EU as the cornerstone of an ambitious attempt to reintegrate British trade back into Europe, the Guardian can reveal.

During recent visits to Brussels, the Cabinet Office’s top official on EU relations, Michael Ellam, presented the idea to deepen the UK’s economic relationship with the bloc.

Continue reading...

events.title: Middle East crisis live: Rubio sees initial progress on reopening Hormuz after Trump claims Iran deal ‘largely negotiated’
events.summary:
The US secretary of state suggested a deal could be announced ‘in the next few hours’ but cautioned it wouldn’t be a ‘final’ agreement

  • Full report: Trump says peace deal with Iran ‘largely negotiated’ with strait of Hormuz to open

In Lebanon, the civil defence agency said early on Sunday its regional facility in the southern city of Nabatieh had been destroyed by an Israeli strike.

The Directorate General of Civil Defence said the building had collapsed and a large number of vehicles and equipment had been damaged by a “direct hit in a hostile Israeli strike”.

Continue reading...

events.title: Trump claims peace deal with Iran ‘largely negotiated’ with strait of Hormuz to open
events.summary:
President’s claim is tempered by Pakistani PM’s hopes of hosting more talks ‘very soon’, and Iran’s Fars news agency saying strait of Hormuz will remain under Iranian control

  • Middle East crisis: live updates

Donald Trump claimed on Saturday that a peace deal with Iran “has been largely negotiated”, after calls with a Pakistani mediator, Gulf allies and Israel, potentially paving the way for an end to the war launched by the US and Israel in February.

Trump wrote on his social media platform that “final aspects and details” of a “memorandum of understanding” were still being discussed and “will be announced shortly”, but said the strait of Hormuz would be opened as part of the deal.

Continue reading...

events.title: Trump to meet with US negotiators to decide on Iran’s ceasefire proposal
events.summary:
US president says it’s a ‘solid 50/50’ on either making a ‘good’ deal with Iran or striking the country anew

Donald Trump said he would meet today with American negotiators to review Iran’s latest proposal and decide by Sunday whether he will strike Iran “to kingdom come”.

The US secretary of state, Marco Rubio, also told reporters in India on Saturday that “there may be news later today” about Iran. He did not specify what that news would be.

Continue reading...

events.title: Israeli bombardment reduces buildings to craters in southern Lebanon
events.summary:
People killed in strikes across multiple locations after child and six paramedics die in attacks a day earlier

Israel has carried out overnight strikes on Lebanon’s Bekaa valley and the southern province of Tyre, killing at least four people and reducing buildings to craters.

The strikes on southern Lebanon continued on Saturday morning, hitting close to Lebanese army barracks, killing a man on a motorcycle in the town of Nabatieh, and killing and wounding people in the town of Chehabiyeh, where the death toll is not yet known.

Continue reading...

events.title: Board of Peace focus on Hamas risks return to war in Gaza, critics say
events.summary:
US-backed board has put sole blame for stalled ceasefire on militant group despite Israel not fulfilling its obligations, analysts say

The top diplomat from the Board of Peace has blamed Hamas for the stalled ceasefire, but critics have said the US-backed board’s lack of even-handedness in implementing the truce risks a return to war.

The “high representative for Gaza”, Nickolay Mladenov, told the UN security council on Thursday that Hamas was the “principal obstacle” to the ceasefire’s continued implementation because “it refused to accept verified decommissioning, relinquish coercive control and allow a genuine civilian transition”.

Continue reading...

events.title: India’s government orders handover of exclusive Delhi Gymkhana Club
events.summary:
Move comes against backdrop of challenge by prime minister, Narendra Modi, to country’s traditional elites

The Indian government has ordered one of the country’s most exclusive private clubs to vacate its premises in two weeks, underscoring the long-running push of the prime minister, Narendra Modi, against entrenched elite institutions.

The ministry of housing and urban affairs directed the Delhi Gymkhana Club to hand over its sprawling site in the heart of New Delhi by 5 June, citing urgent public interest requirements, including defence and security infrastructure.

Continue reading...

events.title: Taliban ‘legitimising child marriage’ with new law, activists warn
events.summary:
Up to 70% of girls may be in early or forced marriages but law now makes divorce impossible if husbands disagree

Child marriage appears to have been legally recognised for the first time by the Taliban in Afghanistan, as activists say “shameful” new laws make it almost impossible for girls and young women to seek divorce against their husbands’ will.

There are no official statistics on forced and underage marriages in Afghanistan, but activists say it has risen at an alarming rate in recent years, driven by the ban on girls being in education after the age of 11.

Continue reading...

events.title: Parody Cockroach Janta political party’s rise reflects youth anger in India
events.summary:
Satirical project is viral sensation and outlet for protest on social media as it taps into young people’s frustration

It began as a satirical online project after India’s chief justice compared unemployed young people to cockroaches. Now millions of young Indians are flocking to it as an outlet for their frustration.

A parody political party with the insect as its symbol has exploded across India’s social media by turning absurdist humour into protest. Memes and short videos mocking corruption, joblessness and political dysfunction have flooded social media sites, where millions of users are embracing the cockroach – an insect known for its ability to survive harsh conditions – as a tongue-in-cheek symbol of endurance.

Continue reading...

events.title: Record 274 climbers summit Everest from Nepalese side in single day
events.summary:
Climbers take advantage of clear weather after threat of ice fall on normal route delayed start of spring season

A record 274 climbers have reached the summit of Mount Everest from the Nepalese side in a single day after a spring season that started late because of the threat of ice fall on the normal tourist route.

The climbers took advantage of the clear weather on Wednesday, said Rishi Ram Bhandari, of the Expedition Operators Association Nepal.

Continue reading...

events.title: US reportedly dropped fraud charges against Indian billionaire after he hired Trump’s lawyer
events.summary:
Gautam Adani, richest man in Asia, was accused of conspiring to pay $250m in bribes to Indian government officials

The US Department of Justice is dropping its fraud charges against the Indian billionaire Gautam Adani, the richest man in Asia, after he hired a new legal team led by Donald Trump’s personal lawyer, according to new reports.

In an undisclosed April meeting at the justice department, Trump’s personal lawyer, Robert J Giuffra Jr, said that Adani would invest $10bn in the US economy and create 15,000 jobs if prosecutors dropped the charges against him, according to the New York Times and Bloomberg.

Continue reading...

events.title: UK judge’s decision not to jail boys for rape like a ‘rock straight in my face’, says victim, 16
events.summary:
Boys, aged 15, given youth rehabilitation orders for two separate attacks against two girls in Hampshire

A judge’s decision not to jail two teenage boys who raped two girls has been described by one of the victims as a “rock straight in my face”.

A trial at Southampton crown court heard the girls were raped in two separate attacks in Fordingbridge, Hampshire, the first attack on 26 November 2024 and the second on 17 January 2025.

Continue reading...

events.title: Treasury rejected ministers’ plan to cut VAT on public EV charging to 5%
events.summary:
Department for Transport is understood to back reducing levy, which critics have called a ‘pavement tax’

  • Business live – latest updates

Government officials considered cutting the VAT charged on electricity used at public EV chargers from 20% to 5% at the last budget, but the Treasury under chancellor Rachel Reeves rejected the proposal amid disagreement between departments.

Officials in the Department for Transport encouraged electric car charge point operators to write to the Treasury explaining how they would respond to a VAT cut, according to three industry sources. The charger companies said that they would pass the tax cut on to consumers.

Continue reading...

events.title: £600 for cheese? The Brazilian beach scams that cost visitors dear
events.summary:
Travellers warned to beware of debit card cons after one was charged £1,500 for a kebab and another £3,000 for corn on the cob

When Lisa Selby* used her debit card to pay for two slices of barbecued cheese from a beach vendor in Rio de Janeiro, she expected to pay 40 reais (£5.90) for the snack.

But shortly after the payment had gone through, she realised that she had been charged 4,000 reais (£590) after the vendor added two extra zeros to the card reader.

Continue reading...

events.title: ‘AI washing’: firms are scrambling to rebrand themselves as tech-focused
events.summary:
PR executives say UK companies are forcing them to present ordinary automation as artificial intelligence

UK companies are performing “yoga-level” stretches to describe themselves as AI specialists in an attempt to capitalise on the buzz around the technology, public relations firms have said.

Weary communications executives tasked with securing media coverage for brands have complained that bosses in low-tech industries or running businesses that use automation but not generative AI, are increasingly demanding they are pitched to journalists as artificial intelligence companies.

Continue reading...

events.title: Squeals of horror over price caps – but how are we going to fix our broken food system?
events.summary:
Global events and the climate crisis have left Britain’s food system dangerously exposed and in desperate need of an overhaul

The news that the Treasury was asking UK supermarkets to cap price rises on essential foods was greeted with predictable squeals of horror this week. Supermarkets were reportedly “furious”, while luminaries from the former head of the Institute for Fiscal Studies to the former chair of M&S could be found harrumphing about the evils of price controls.

But this caterwauling is a distraction from two unpleasant facts. Firstly, the food price surge over the summer and beyond is likely to be significant – and will come on top of a near-40% rise in the price of food since 2020 – due to a devastating combination of the Iran war and a forecast record-breaking El Niño, which will hammer global food production. And secondly, Britain’s food system is painfully exposed to such shocks. The long-held assumption that a global food system can be relied on to meet the nation’s needs, at a reasonable price, no longer applies.

Continue reading...

events.title: Former prosecutor calls for EU statute blocking US sanctions on ICC members
events.summary:
US imposed sanctions on nine judges and a prosecutor after ICC issued arrest warrants for members of Israeli cabinet

A former prosecutor at the international criminal court has called for an EU-wide statute blocking what she describes as “thuggish” and “bullying” US sanctions imposed on members of the court that are designed to send the court into oblivion.

In February 2025, the US imposed sanctions on 11 ICC officials, including nine judges and the chief prosecutor as well three Palestinian organisations, in response to the ICC decision in 2024 to issue arrest warrants for members of the Israeli cabinet, including the prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu.

Continue reading...

events.title: Stephen Colbert hosts Michigan public access show day after last appearance on Late Show
events.summary:
Guests on Only in Monroe included Jack White and Jeff Daniels, with appearances by Steve Buscemi and Eminem

After his last appearance on CBS as host of The Late Show, Stephen Colbert continued his television hosting duties – on a public access show in Michigan.

Colbert on Friday night hosted Only in Monroe in Monroe, Michigan, just one day after signing off from The Late Show. Colbert had been the host of The Late Show since 2015 and his tenure came to an end this week after CBS canceled the 33-year-old network staple, due to a “financial decision” by CBS’s parent company, Paramount.

Continue reading...

events.title: Three dead and 18 first responders sickened by apparent fentanyl exposure in New Mexico
events.summary:
First responders hospitalized and decontaminated after four people were found apparently overdosing

Three people died and 18 first responders were sickened by exposure to fentanyl in a rural New Mexico home this week, New Mexico state police said.

Police initially found four people unconscious at the home in Mountainair, 90 miles (145km) east of Albuquerque. Two were declared dead at the scene and a third died at a hospital.

Continue reading...

events.title: Trump's justice department scrubs its website of news releases about January 6 defendants
events.summary:
Department of Justice acknowledges removal of information about criminal cases related to 2021 US Capitol rioters

The Department of Justice is acknowledging it has removed from its website news releases about criminal cases related to the 6 January 2021 Capitol attack, calling the information about the prosecutions “partisan propaganda”.

The purge of news releases documenting criminal charges, convictions and sentencings is the latest step by the Trump administration to dramatically rewrite the history of the assault on the US Capitol, when hundreds of supporters of Donald Trump stormed the building in an effort to halt the congressional certification of his 2020 election loss to Democrat Joe Biden.

Continue reading...

events.title: How big tech got its way on Trump’s AI executive order
events.summary:
The US president’s reversal on calling for a safety review of new AI models is a green light for tech’s unchecked power

Only hours before Donald Trump was set to sign a long-awaited executive order on Thursday that would have called for a government safety review of new artificial intelligence models before their release, the president abruptly backed out. Despite growing public backlash to the technology and experts warning new models will pose critical security risks, Trump vowed the US government would not slow down the AI race.

During a meeting with reporters on Thursday, Trump cited both American dominance and competition with China and as his reasoning behind the reversal.

Continue reading...

events.title: Romanian director Cristian Mungiu's 'Fjord' wins top prize at Cannes
events.summary: Mungiu took home the prestigious Palme D'Or for his film Fjord, a culture-war drama set in Norway.

events.title: Trump says a deal with Iran and opening of Strait of Hormuz are 'largely negotiated'
events.summary: President Trump announced the unfinalized deal on social media after talks with Israel and other allies. It aims to end the war and includes two-month negotiations on Iran's nuclear program.

events.title: The squeeze on Cuba now includes compensation lawsuits
events.summary: American University professor William LeoGrande explains how the Supreme Court's decision to allow lawsuits seeking compensation for assets seized in the Cuban revolution to move forward fits in context of current political crisis on the island.

events.title: Severe global food crisis could come within the year, says UN agency
events.summary: Maximo Torero, Chief Economist at The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations, warns that the war in Iran is choking the global supply of fertilizer and a food crisis could follow within a year.

events.title: Video shows Israeli 'double-tap strike' killed medics and a toddler in Lebanon
events.summary: In Lebanon, an Israeli 'double tap' killed three medics and four others including a two-year-old girl. A neighbor's video shows what happened.

events.title: What will Trump do next with Iran?
events.summary: Three months since the start of its conflict with Iran, the Trump administration is oscillating between strike threats and diplomatic overtures. Neither path has yielded a clear resolution.

events.title: U.S. passengers flying from Ebola-affected countries rerouted
events.summary: The U.S. government is responding to the Ebola outbreak in with travel restrictions. American citizens and permanent residents departing affected countries must fly into one of three U.S. airports.

events.title: NATO allies confused by Trump's unexpected move to deploy 5,000 U.S. troops to Poland
events.summary: Some NATO allies say they are confused by President Trump's different statements on U.S. troop deployments to Europe.

events.title: Chile's MAGA-inspired border control
events.summary: Chile digs desert trenches along its northern border as President José Antonio Kast pushes a hardline migration crackdown critics say may have little effect.

events.title: Deadliest coal mine explosion in China in years kills at least 82 people
events.summary: A gas explosion at a coal mine in China's northern province of Shanxi killed at least 82 people. Official news agency Xinhua said the accident happened on Friday evening. Around 247 workers were on duty at the time.

events.title: Philippines: 20 feared trapped after building collapse
events.summary: The building collapsed before dawn after a severe thunderstorm. Officials said rescuers reported "hearing voices" in the rubble.

events.title: Germany news: Most doubt Bundeswehr military readiness — poll
events.summary: A new poll shows widespread doubts about Germany's defense readiness, with stronger fears over cyber threats than military attack. Meanwhile, a record number of people obtained a German passport in 2025. DW has more.

events.title: Australia: Man dies of shark attack on eastern coast
events.summary: The man was bitten by a shark at Kennedy Shoal in Australia's northeastern state of Queensland, in the second shark attack in the country this month.

events.title: Displaced Ukrainians risk home seizure under Russian law
events.summary: Russia is tightening control over property in occupied Ukrainian territories, requiring owners to register under Russian law. Many Ukrainians say returning to do so would put their safety and freedom at risk.

events.title: Enhanced Games: Why 'crippling injuries' are the least of it
events.summary: Doping in sports is nothing new. Athletes at the Enhanced Games will know the risks — severe injury, even paralysis. But bioethicists are also concerned we're opening the gates to altering our fundamental human biology.

events.title: Missiles hit Kyiv after Russia vows retaliation
events.summary: Russia struck Kyiv with a powerful hypersonic Oreshnik ballistic missile, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said.

events.title: Iran war: Donald Trump says deal 'largely negotiated'
events.summary: The US president also said the draft agreement, which hasn't been confirmed, includes the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. But Iran says Trump's claims are "incomplete and ⁠inconsistent ​with reality." DW has more.

events.title: US: Suspect dies after gunfire near White House
events.summary: There was a massive police response after gunshots were fired near the White House, where President Donald Trump was working on a deal with Iran. The suspect has been shot and killed by law enforcement.

events.title: Kane's hat trick seals Bayern Munich's 21st German Cup win
events.summary: Harry Kane scored three times to fire Bayern Munich to a 3-0 victory over Stuttgart in the German Cup final. The win delivered a domestic double for the Bavarian giants and ended a six-year trophy drought.

events.title: Serbia: Police clash with protesters urging early elections
events.summary: Officers fired teargas and stun grenades as tens of thousands rallied against President Aleksandar Vucic's government. The demonstration went ahead despite the state rail operator canceling trains to and from Belgrade.

events.title: Thousands rally in Madrid to demand Sanchez's resignation
events.summary: Mounting corruption allegations involving Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez’s family, allies and party sparked the protest.

events.title: Cyprus election expected to shake up national politics
events.summary: Cyprus goes to the polls to elect a new parliament on Sunday. The vote is expected to reshape the country's political landscape, weaken traditional parties and accelerate the rise of new anti-establishment movements.

events.title: Cheap attack drones break through Israel's Iron Dome
events.summary: Inexpensive fiber-optic drones are challenging Israel's high-tech defenses, shifting the military balance in the Middle East.

events.title: Couple remanded in custody after boys found abandoned by road in Portugal
events.summary: A French woman is accused of abandoning her children in Portugal after reportedly blindfolding them and leading them into a wooded area during a game. Her French partner also faces charges.

events.title: US, Iran strike upbeat tone as Hormuz deal takes shape
events.summary: US President Donald Trump flagged progress in talks with Iran, Tehran has warned that major gaps still stand in the way of dialing down the conflict.

events.title: In Iran, internet access becomes a luxury for the few
events.summary: Despite the ongoing internet blockade in Iran, access to "Internet-Pro" is available to select groups for a fee. Access to information and communication is increasingly becoming a luxury.

events.title: France bans Israel minister Ben-Gvir after flotilla video
events.summary: The Israeli far-right minister released a video taunting detained Gaza aid flotilla activists, prompting widespread criticism at home and internationally.

events.title: What is Pentecost?
events.summary: People new to Germany are often surprised by the number of public holidays in the spring. Pentecost is one of them, but its significance is not well-known.

events.title: Thailand revives bill to clear the air of toxic smog
events.summary: Seasonal smog is a major public health problem in Thailand. Long-delayed legislation aims to stop air pollution before it starts, but enforcement could present a problem.

events.title: Ukrainian strike in Russian-controlled area leaves 10 dead
events.summary: Russian authorities in Luhansk said a Ukrainian drone hit a university dorm, wounding dozens and killing 10. Vladimir Putin has condemned the drone barrage and ordered the army to prepare a response.

events.title: Germany: Record numbers take German passport in 2025
events.summary: A record number of people received a German passport in 2025, a media report says. Numbers have been rising since Germany changed citizenship requirements in 2024.

events.title: Czechia: Uproar over Sudeten German gathering in Brno
events.summary: The first gathering of Sudeten Germans in Czechia since World War II is happening this weekend, despite a political backlash that has exposed tensions over one of Central Europe's most painful historical legacies.

events.title: Germany's 'rapid militarization' threatens basic rights
events.summary: Ten human rights organizations say security interests are increasingly being prioritized over fundamental rights in Germany. The scathing criticism was presented in their "2026 Fundamental Rights Report."

events.title: Kevin Warsh: Trump ally at the Federal Reserve?
events.summary: Never before has a chair of the US Federal Reserve been so controversial. Who is Kevin Warsh? Can he preserve the Fed's independence, or will he become Donald Trump's puppet?

events.title: US or Iran: Who will win the Hormuz endurance game?
events.summary: Tehran is betting that its sanctions‑hardened economy can outlast the Strait of Hormuz crisis. Washington, meanwhile, is contending with elevated oil prices, renewed inflation pressure and rising global recession risks.

events.title: The dying dream of the dead to be at peace with life
events.summary: Many people have vivid dreams shortly before death. Research suggests the dreams are not a sign of confusion — but may help both the dying and their loved ones make sense of loss.

events.title: What happened to jailed Belarus dissident Raman Pratasevich?
events.summary: In 2021, Belarus diverted a Ryanair plane to seize opposition journalist Raman Pratasevich. What has happened to him since, and why is he now seemingly supporting the Belarusian regime?

events.title: How the Cannes 2026 films reflect a world in conflict
events.summary: Various works explore the impact of authoritarianism through a historical lens, while one thriller on corruption is set in Putin's Russia.

events.title: DW exclusive: Germany's far-right AfD vows to 'make history'
events.summary: The AfD looks set to take power in a German state for the first time. In a DW exclusive, the front-runner in Saxony-Anhalt discusses plans to detain immigrants and revamp the school system, and relations with Russia.

events.title: Ebola patient in Berlin: Why Germany's helping
events.summary: An Ebola‑infected US doctor is being treated under the highest safety standards in a specialized isolation unit in the German capital, Berlin. The facilities are considered to be among the best equipped worldwide.

events.title: Will Germany's aviation tax cut lower ticket prices?
events.summary: The tax cut aims to ease the burden on the aviation industry and make flying slightly cheaper. However, some experts warn that it is unlikely to have a meaningful impact on ticket prices.

events.title: Germany reaches out to Gulf states over Iran war
events.summary: Germany no longer believes it can play a mediating role in the conflict between the US, Israel and Iran. Instead, the German government is working to strengthen its ties with the Gulf states.

events.title: Turkey: DW's Alican Uludag released, but trial continues
events.summary: A Turkish court has allowed DW reporter Alican Uludag to leave detention as his contentious trial opens. He is accused of having "insulted" President Recep Tayyip Erdogan, which in Turkey can carry a jail sentence.

events.title: Will SpaceX's IPO fund life on Mars — and a trillionaire?
events.summary: SpaceX is launching history's biggest public listing, propelling Elon Musk's wealth into the stratosphere. DW explores whether the firm's out-of-this-world ambitions are truly cosmic or another black hole for investors.

events.title: Latvia scrambles NATO jets in drone alert, the latest of several in Baltic
events.summary: Latvia's military said NATO planes responded to at least one drone flying in its airspace. It's the latest in a string of such incidents in the Baltic, one of which eventually toppled Latvia's government.

events.title: Germany 2026 World Cup squad announcement: Neuer returns
events.summary: Two years after retiring from international football, Manuel Neuer is back in Germany's 2026 World Cup squad.

events.title: Extreme climate scenario fades, but warming continues
events.summary: The rapid rollout of renewable energy is helping to shift emissions trends, but expected temperature rises remain high as the UN moves to tighten countries' commitments.

events.title: Montenegro at 20: EU ambitions and Serbian ties
events.summary: Twenty years after seceding from the state union with Serbia, Montenegro is seen as a Western Balkan frontrunner for EU membership but remains marked by corruption, unresolved divisions and Serbia's political influence.

events.title: Freiburg's Europa League final proof another way can work
events.summary: Bundesliga side Freiburg might have lost their first-ever European final to Aston Villa, but their European run is a reminder that success in football can be achieved without big spending or a loss of identity.

events.title: Germany records high numbers of online child abuse cases
events.summary: Efforts to regulate social media have had little effect so far, according to Jugendschutz.net. The government-funded organization says young people in Germany are defenseless against online hate and violence.

events.title: German authorities arrest 2 on China high-tech espionage allegations
events.summary: Two Munich residents have been arrested on suspicion of working for a Chinese intelligence service, seeking to obtain and pass on high-tech information with "military applications."

events.title: Turkey targets press freedom in trial of DW's Alican Uludag
events.summary: DW correspondent Alican Uludag has been in custody in Turkey for three months, in part on charges of "insulting the president." He is set to appear in court for the first time on Thursday, though only via video link.

events.title: Why Stephen Colbert's 'Late Show' mattered
events.summary: As the final curtain falls on "The Late Show," a brief history of the evolution of the late-night tradition and Stephen Colbert's satire.

events.title: Researchers spot humpback whales off Australia and Brazil — longest lifetime journey on record
events.summary: Researchers have observed two humpback whales swimming between Australia and Brazil. It's the longest travel distance humans have ever recorded for the species.

events.title: How Meta wants to profile 13-year-olds on Insta, Facebook
events.summary: Instagram and Facebook have a problem with underage users — there's too many of them. Parent company, Meta, aims to root out under-13s with an age-profiling AI. This plays into a debate about a teenage social media ban.

events.title: EU is failing to cut pesticide use
events.summary: With binding cut targets scrapped, the European Union's pledge to halve pesticide use is stalling — even as controversial chemicals like glyphosate remain on sale across the bloc.

events.title: Enhanced Games: Pushing human potential, or lethal circus?
events.summary: Investors like Peter Thiel and Donald Trump Jr. see the Enhanced Games as proof of how far human limits can stretch. For doping experts, the danger is clear and grave. DW explains the latest controversial sporting event.

events.title: Russian President Vladimir Putin in Beijing for talks with Chinese leader Xi Jinping
events.summary: Presidents Xi Jinping and Vladimir Putin each praised their countries' bilateral relations during the Russian leader's visit to Beijing.

events.title: Hungary's new Prime Minister Magyar seeks to fix Poland ties
events.summary: Peter Magyar hopes to mend relations with Poland during his first state visit to Warsaw. What lessons could he learn from Poland's Prime Minister Donald Tusk?

events.title: Pioneer of pop music: Cher turns 80
events.summary: Cher has had countless hits and sparked more than a few controversies — while always doing things her own way. At 80, she remains as iconic as ever.

events.title: Germany beefs up civil defense
events.summary: Germany's government wants to spend €10 billion to better protect its population in case of emergencies. The backlog is huge.

events.title: NATO planes down suspected Ukrainian drone over Estonia
events.summary: A NATO military jet has shot down a suspected Ukrainian drone in Estonian airspace. As Ukraine targets Russia's Baltic oil facilities more often and more intensely, more such drones are straying into allies' territory.

events.title: Hearing aids may cut noise by reading brain signals
events.summary: In a crowded room, it can be impossible for hearing aid users to focus on what they want or need to hear. Research into auditory neurotechnology may enhance sounds and voices and reduce noise in real-time.

events.title: Sweden orders 4 frigates from French company Naval Group
events.summary: Sweden plans to acquire four new frigates starting in 2030 in a deal worth billions. They will be the largest surface warships in Sweden's navy. The newest NATO member is rushing to build up its military capabilities.

events.title: How China became Russia's economic lifeline
events.summary: Hot on the heels of Donald Trump's visit, Russian President Vladimir Putin travels to Beijing for talks with Xi Jinping. DW explores how China's trade and financial support is helping keep Russia's economy afloat.

events.title: Germany news: 2030 climate goals in danger, experts warn
events.summary: Headlines in Germany this Monday include higher than planned CO2 emissions, plans to ramp up civil defense measures, and Chancellor Merz meeting euroskeptic Bulgarian Premier Rumen Radev.

events.title: Germany's crisis-hit chemical industry seeks revival
events.summary: Hit by high energy costs and regulation, Germany's chemical companies are scaling back production at home while expanding abroad. Can the trend be reversed?

events.title: Elversberg: Bundesliga newcomers from tiny town make history
events.summary: A team from a small southwestern German town of just 7,000 inhabitants has made history by sealing promotion to become the 59th club to play in the Bundesliga.

events.title: Ebola in Africa: Why it's a constant threat
events.summary: Ebola is highly contagious and deadly. It spreads internationally, such as in the May 2026 outbreaks in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda. But that doesn't automatically make it a pandemic.

events.title: Young Czechs drink less beer but risk other addictions
events.summary: The Czech Republic, the beer world champion, has seen a record decline in consumption. Younger generations are drinking less. Breweries are worried, but health experts say it's good news.

events.title: German singer Heino pushes back against the far-right AfD
events.summary: A far‑right politician in Germany used schlager icon Heino in election advertising without his consent. Heino is now demanding compensation for pain and suffering.

events.title: Tiger escapes enclosure near Leipzig, one person injured
events.summary: An escaped tiger has injured a 73-year-old man near Leipzig, with police saying they shot the animal. German media reported the tiger was owned by a controversial performer.

events.title: Germany and Japan test hydrogen future with BMW, Toyota cars
events.summary: During a recent visit to Japan, Germany's transport minister Patrick Schnieder toured hydrogen projects aimed at bringing the fuel into the mainstream.

events.title: Germany news: UNICEF says child well-being ranking 'alarming'
events.summary: A new study ranks Germany near the bottom in child well-being and education. Meanwhile, an escaped tiger was killed by police after injuring a man near Leipzig. DW has more.

events.title: Dead humpback whale off Denmark is 'Timmy'
events.summary: Divers have confirmed that a dead humpback whale spotted off Denmark is the same creature that spent weeks beached off Germany's Baltic coast. It comes two weeks after a contentious rescue operation.

events.title: Germany stakes claim in new space race
events.summary: German companies of all sizes and all over the country are developing satellites and space applications. They want to be part of the lucrative industry, which is increasingly crucial for civilian and military purposes.

events.title: Could Romania and Moldova reunite?
events.summary: Moldovan leader Maia Sandu and her Romanian counterpart Nicusor Dan want to reunite their countries. Is that a realistic scenario?

events.title: Germany's housing crisis hits immigrants hardest
events.summary: The German housing market crisis is deepening social inequality. Newcomers in particular are struggling to find affordable housing — with consequences for integration, education, and the labor market.

events.title: 'From collection to connection': How museums are redefining themselves
events.summary: Gone are the days when museums were just places to see art and artifacts. On International Museum Day, DW looks at how museums are becoming unique spaces of community engagement and participation.

events.title: Andreas Rettig: 'You can't separate sports and politics'
events.summary: The World Cup in the USA, Canada and Mexico is sparking discussions beyond the realm of football. DFB Director Andreas Rettig discusses the Germany team and the geopolitical situation in an interview with DW.

events.title: Germany news: Chancellor Merz says he wouldn't advise his kids to go to the US
events.summary: Chancellor Friedrich Merz said he wouldn't currently advise young Germans like his children to move to the US to study or work. He cited "the social climate that has suddenly developed."

events.title: Islamic feminism more widespread in Bosnia and Herzegovina
events.summary: Many Bosnian Muslim women want to attend mosque on Fridays and to play a greater role in decision-making. Slowly the official structures of the Islamic Community are changing.

events.title: Ukraine: Dozens of European countries sign up to special tribunal plan to prosecute Russia
events.summary: At talks in Moldova, more than 30 countries have said they will join a future special tribunal for Ukraine prosecuting Russia over its invasion. How the body will force Russians to stand trial is not clear.

events.title: UK politics: How Starmer survives as Farage waits in wings
events.summary: High profile resignations, a prime minister on the edge and populists on the up — UK politics is far from dull. DW explains what's going on in Keir Starmer's Labour party and with Nigel Farage. And what may be next.

events.title: Iconic German musician Udo Lindenberg turns 80
events.summary: From a historic East German concert to a hit with rapper Apache 207, Udo Lindenberg has shaped German rock music like few others. Even at 80, the "Panikrocker" is still reinventing himself.

events.title: Asteroid 2026 JH2 — no need to worry about it hitting Earth
events.summary: This big asteroid was discovered shortly before it was expected to hurtle past Earth. Any time you see a headline about asteroids approaching the planet, you can relax. Big and small asteroids fly by all the time.

events.title: 'FIFA got greedy' ― Why football fans in India may not be able to watch World Cup games on TV this year
events.summary: The 2026 World Cup is weeks away, but one of the most populated countries in the world doesn't have a broadcasting deal. Why?

events.title: Foreign student fee hikes in France trigger backlash
events.summary: A French government plan to charge students from outside the European Union 16 times more than previously has stoked discontent. But how does this compare to the rest of Europe, both inside the EU and out?

events.title: Will Ukraine corruption scandal reach Volodymyr Zelenskyy?
events.summary: A drawn-out corruption affair in Ukraine has widened, with authorities now taking action against Andriy Yermak, former head of the presidential office. Will the scandal reach all the way to President Volodymyr Zelenskyy?

events.title: UK: Health Secretary Wes Streeting resigns, likely in bid to topple PM Keir Starmer
events.summary: Two Labour Party politicians have positioned themselves to challenge Prime Minister Keir Starmer for the leadership. Health Secretary Wes Streeting resigned, and Angela Rayner said her tax affairs were back in order.

events.title: Lab-grown Tyrannosaurus leather: More chicken than dinosaur?
events.summary: A handbag is promoted as the "world's first T. rex leather product". But scientists are questioning its authenticity.

events.title: German intelligence offices snub US-based Palantir software
events.summary: Germany's domestic intelligence agency has reportedly chosen a data analysis system from France, instead of US-based Palantir. Civil society has welcomed the move, but says more must be done.

events.title: Who is Mario Draghi, this year's Charlemagne Prize winner?
events.summary: The Charlemagne Prize honors those who advance European unity. This year's winner is Italy's Mario Draghi, a former European Central Bank chief and a key architect of European stability during a time of economic crisis.

events.title: Honda reports first loss since 1957 as it waters down EV strategy, but shares rise on 2026 forecasts
events.summary: The Japanese carmaker pursued an aggressive switch towards electric vehicles that it's now trying to walk back. Despite posting its first loss since 1957, its share price spiked on more buoyant predictions for 2026.

events.title: China goes electric, but can it get off coal?
events.summary: The world's clean energy superpower also emits the most CO2. But coal-fired power may have peaked as China electrifies its economy with ever more renewables.

events.title: US drops bond requirement for FIFA World Cup ticket holders
events.summary: Fans from five African World Cup qualifying nations with valid tickets are exempt from paying a hefty bond. But fans from several other countries still face major hurdles to seeing their teams play in the United States.

events.title: Germany's shortfall in medium-range defense capabilities
events.summary: The US has announced that it will not be deploying Tomahawk cruise missiles in Germany. Now the German government is looking for alternatives. Could drones be part of the solution?

events.title: Fake TikTok fans and pop music's 'unethical' marketing
events.summary: Do indie darlings like Sombr and Geese owe their success to TikTok "trend simulations"? The question has sent shockwaves through their fan base. Yet manipulated charts are as old as the music business itself.

events.title: China’s new rules give the West a new headache
events.summary: Beijing now has strong new powers to punish firms for pulling manufacturing out of China. Multinationals are increasingly caught in a web of coercion and complex rules as they try to operate across the US, EU and China.

events.title: Satellites add a new layer to global poverty data
events.summary: A new study using satellite data finds that 58% of the global population has been assigned to the wrong tier in the UN's Human Development Index. That can have real-world consequences for how aid reaches people.

events.title: Why men are less worried than women about climate change
events.summary: From petro-masculinity to electric trucks: why men disengage from climate action and what might bring them back.

events.title: Hotter 2026 and El Nino could trigger extreme fires
events.summary: More than 150 million hectares — over twice the size of Texas — burned globally in the first months of 2026. With a high chance of a supercharged El Nino, the second half of the year could be even worse.

events.title: Bundesliga: Kathleen Krüger appointed Hamburg's head of sport
events.summary: Hamburg have appointed Kathleen Krüger as the club's new board member responsible for sport. She becomes the first woman to fill such a role at a Bundesliga club on a permanent basis.

events.title: Sport helping displaced Lebanese living in stadium to cope
events.summary: More than a million people have been forced to flee their homes in southern Lebanon due to fighting between Israeli forces and Hezbollah. As many shelter in Beirut stadiums, sport is sometimes a welcome distraction.

events.title: Hijab-wearing German beauty contestant takes on the AfD
events.summary: As a Miss Germany contestant, Büsra Sayed wore a hijab on stage. This had never happened before — and the far-right AfD didn't like it. Her response went viral.

events.title: Hantavirus: How is the outbreak being contained?
events.summary: Passengers evacuated from the MV Hondius cruise ship are returning to their home countries, some with symptoms, many without. What happens to these people and what is being done to contain the spread?

events.title: Architecture of remembrance and hope: Daniel Libeskind at 80
events.summary: Famous for designing the Jewish Museum Berlin and the Ground Zero project in New York, the architect has created dozens of memorable buildings worldwide.

events.title: Hantavirus: How it differs from COVID
events.summary: In 2018-2019, the Andes virus hantavirus spread person-to-person in Argentina. Eleven people died — but it was stopped. Analysis of that outbreak shows the current one can also be stopped.

events.title: Germany news: 1-in-6 retailers fear for future
events.summary: About one-in-12 German businesses worry about survival, with twice as many retailers jittery. Meanwhile in Stuttgart, five pro-Palestinian activists face trial over attacking an Israeli defense company site. Follow DW.

events.title: Endemic, epidemic, pandemic: What's the difference?
events.summary: The Andes hantavirus outbreak shows how easily disease labels are misunderstood — and why they describe patterns of spread, not how dangerous a virus is.

events.title: Somali piracy disrupts global shipping and trade routes
events.summary: As ships reroute around Africa to avoid Middle East conflict zones, piracy off Somalia is back. The resurgence is driving up insurance, transit times and security costs, putting new pressure on global supply chains.

events.title: Why is Hollywood missing from Cannes?
events.summary: There are no US blockbusters premiering at the Cannes Film Festival this year. So who's snubbing who?

events.title: New film portrays Thomas and Erika Mann — with an oddly twisted premise
events.summary: Premiering at Cannes and starring Sandra Hüller, Pawel Pawlikowski's "Fatherland" is set during the Cold War — a complex period for the prominent anti-Nazi intellectuals.

events.title: Eurovision 2026: Politics collide with a blockbuster show
events.summary: Controversy over Israel's participation and boycotts unprecedented in scale are already weighing on the Eurovision Song Contest before it even kicks off in Vienna.

events.title: Celebrated broadcaster and naturalist David Attenborough turns 100
events.summary: The still-sprightly centenarian has been knighted twice, nominated for the 2022 Nobel Peace Prize, and is perhaps the world's most recognizable nature lover.

events.title: World Cup 'prices will drop' but too late for traveling fans
events.summary: Among many firsts at World Cup 2026, FIFA has tried its hand at dynamic pricing and acting as a secondary seller. The tactic has seen a ticket listed for over $2 million, but many games seem undersold. What's going on?

events.title: Why the EU sees Chinese solar tech as a major security risk
events.summary: Europe's solar boom is powered by Chinese technology. But experts warn Chinese-made tech could threaten the continent's safety and even create blackouts. Now, Brussels is aiming to reduce its dependence.

events.title: Bayern Munich exit Champions League in semifinal to PSG
events.summary: Holders Paris Saint-Germain are one game away from retaining the Champions League after knocking out German champions Bayern Munich. Bayern can still complete the domestic double but again miss out on the big one.

events.title: COVID pioneer BioNTech: The fall of Germany's pandemic hero
events.summary: BioNTech, once celebrated for its COVID-19 vaccines, is grappling with falling demand, restructuring and the departure of its founders. Can its ambitious cancer trials turn it from a one-hit wonder into a biotech giant?

events.title: Venice Biennale in crisis: The controversies explained
events.summary: Following the resignation of the jury over Russia's and Israel's disputed participation, the traditional Golden Lion will not be awarded this year.

events.title: Hungary's new government pushes for euro by 2030
events.summary: Hungary's new leadership wants the country to adopt the euro by the end of the decade while repairing strained ties with Brussels. But with a weak economy and tight deadlines, experts warn the path will be steep.

events.title: What the Strait of Hormuz fuel crisis means for air travel
events.summary: Does travel insurance cover flight cancellations caused by the Iran war? Travelers could face major disruption as the jet fuel crisis threatens summer flights.

events.title: How Rosalia became a global pop force
events.summary: From a small town in Spain to sold-out global arenas, Spanish singer Rosalia has built her career with precision rather than luck. How did she do it?

events.title: What is hantavirus?
events.summary: Three people have died on a cruise ship in the Atlantic, reportedly from hantavirus. The dangerous Andes strain has been confirmed in two cases. What are the symptoms, and how is it transmitted?

events.title: The sea that is vanishing in real time
events.summary: The world's largest inland body of water is retreating at an alarming speed. From stranded buildings to vanishing habitats, scientists warn the Caspian Sea may be approaching a tipping point.

events.title: From India to Iran: How Hitler redefined 'Aryan' for the Nazis
events.summary: According to Nazi ideology, an ideal "Aryan" was blond, blue-eyed with athletic features. The term is still tied to Nazi Germany, but its origin lies elsewhere.

events.title: Dubai's image as a financial hub faces its biggest test yet
events.summary: Amid the smoldering Iran war, wealthy elites are quietly shifting capital and other assets out of Dubai and heading to Singapore and Switzerland. Just how resilient is the emirate's safe-haven reputation?

events.title: Why Saudi Arabia is withdrawing from sport
events.summary: There are few major sports left that are not supported by investment from Saudi Arabia. But with the kingdom pulling out of golf and canceling planned events and contracts elsewhere, is the Saudi sports era over?

events.title: Georg Baselitz, artist renowned for his upside-down paintings, dies
events.summary: He was thrown out of art school at the age of 18 because of his love for Picasso. The German artist who never lost his rebellious streak has died at the age of 88.

events.title: Angine de Poitrine: A masked rock band conquers the internet
events.summary: From underground experiment to sold-out international concerts within days — as the French-Canadian band Angine de Poitrine starts touring the world, we take a look at the viral phenomenon.

events.title: Women's Champions League: Bayern fall to Barcelona in semis
events.summary: Bayern Munich have come up short in their bid to win the Women's Champions League for the first time after losing to Barcelona in the semifinals.

events.title: Millions sway as free Shakira concert lights up Copacabana
events.summary: The Colombian superstar delivers one of the biggest shows of her career, as fans flock from Brazil and beyond for her "Women No Longer Cry" world tour.

events.title: Alex Zanardi, F1 driver and multiple CART and Paralympic champion, dies at 59
events.summary: Italian race driver turned Paralympian Alex Zanardi has died aged 59. The former Formula 1 driver and two-time CART champion lost his legs in a horrific crash at the peak of his powers, but his drive to compete endured.

events.title: Gender investment gap: Are women catching up with men?
events.summary: Online brokers are tapping into the potential of female investors. Tailor-made products and branding campaigns are on the rise. Will this help bridge the gap between women and men when it comes to investing?

events.title: Iran's Vafaei playing for more than snooker glory
events.summary: After years lost to visa battles, Hossein Vafaei is finally delivering on his early promise as a snooker prodigy. He's hoping to inspire people back home amid the turmoil in Iran.

events.title: Study busts myth: German 'spring fatigue' doesn't exist
events.summary: Do we really feel more tired in spring? Researchers in Switzerland say, "there's no such thing as spring fatigue." But it is a deep cultural phenomenon in German-speaking countries.

events.title: How widespread is global fatigue with the US?
events.summary: The global image of the US has taken a blow, as many people are increasingly fed up with the constant stream of controversies surrounding the Trump administration.

events.title: A new climate club, minus the fossil fuel lobby
events.summary: Frustrated by blocking coalitions, 50-plus nations at the inaugural talks on exiting fossil fuels made one thing clear: it's all about how — not whether — to phase out coal, oil and gas.

events.title: Will EU bring in a windfall tax on oil companies?
events.summary: Five countries, including Germany, want the EU to bring in a so-called windfall tax to target energy companies benefiting from the price crisis. It can be done, but it's complex.

events.title: Why UAE's OPEC exit is a blow to Saudi Arabia
events.summary: The United Arab Emirates is leaving OPEC to pump more oil on its own terms. The break strips Saudi Arabia of a key partner and adds to growing uncertainty over the cartel's future.

events.title: Football: Afghan women's team recognized in blow to Taliban
events.summary: Banned from playing football and sent into exile, Afghanistan's women's team has had to fight to regain international status. That newly awarded recognition is a "hard slap to the face" of the Taliban, a player told DW.

events.title: 2026 Goethe Medal: Meet the recipients
events.summary: Though they work in different fields and countries, what unites them is their focus on a Europe in transition: Arvo Pärt, Anita Raja and Prodromos Tsinikoris have been awarded the 2026 Goethe Medal.

events.title: Extreme weather and green energy on the rise in Europe
events.summary: Europe's climate extremes have hit new highs — but renewables are now supplying nearly half of the continent's electricity.

events.title: Germany's entrepreneurs betting on post-Maduro Venezuela
events.summary: Venezuela's economy is opening sectors to foreign firms, and that could pay off for the few German companies that have stuck it out through years of decline.

events.title: What Renault's new EV reveals about the global auto industry
events.summary: French carmaker Renault no longer sells its brand in the Chinese market, but it's not afraid to design and develop them there. It's just one more sign of big shifts in the global auto industry.

events.title: Bundesliga games abroad unlikely, but for how long?
events.summary: New proposals from FIFA will allow domestic leagues to host one game abroad a season. As a result, Europe's football leagues could face a big change sooner rather than later.

events.title: 'Marine unicorns' aren't loving Arctic noise
events.summary: Narwhals are fleeing Canada's far north. Researchers suspect a link to noise pollution from increasing ship traffic.

events.title: Will the Iran war end Strait of Hormuz oil supremacy?
events.summary: The conflict has brutally exposed the energy market's Achilles' heel. While global powers China, India and the EU push renewables, Gulf leaders are advancing plans for new bypass pipelines to safeguard their oil empires.

events.title: Sawe smashes 2-hour mark setting record at London Marathon
events.summary: Kenya's Sabastian Sawe has made history in London as the first man to run a marathon in under two hours. He beat the world record by more than a minute.

events.title: US secret service kills gunman near White House, President Trump says suspect had ‘violent history’
Despite Middle East war, foreign pilgrims flock to Mecca for hajj
events.summary: Despite the war in the Middle East, foreign pilgrims have flocked to Mecca to participate in this year’s hajj. According to officials, more than 1.5 million have already arrived in Saudi Arabia, surpassing last year’s numbers.

events.title: Djokovic headlines opening day at French Open
events.summary: Novak Djokovic is headlining the first day of the French Open on Sunday where he will face France's Giovanni Mpetshi Perricard on centre court. The 39-year-old Serb has already won Roland Garros three times and another title would give him a record-breaking 25 Grand Slam crowns.

events.title: Middle East war live: Rubio says announcement on Iran war possible later Sunday
events.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Sunday said an announcement on the Iran war could be possible later in the day. While US media has reported that a deal would include a commitment from Tehran to hand over its stockpile of highly enriched uranium, an Iranian source told Reuters this was not the case. Follow our liveblog for the latest developments.

events.title: Several dead, dozens injured in massive Russian drone and missile attack on Kyiv region
events.summary: At least four people have been killed and dozens more have been injured in a massive Russian drone and missile attack Sunday on Ukraine’s capital Kyiv and its surrounding region, officials said. Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelensky has confirmed that an ‌Oreshnik hypersonic ⁠intermediate-range ballistic ‌missile was used in the strikes.

events.title: Gunman killed near White House, Trump says suspect had ‘history of violence’
events.summary: A gunman opened fire near the White House on Saturday evening before being shot dead by US Secret Service agents in a confrontation that also left a bystander wounded. President Donald Trump, who was inside the White House at the time, was not harmed in the incident.

events.title: 'Fjord' starring Sebastian Stan wins the Palme d’Or at 2026 Cannes Film Festival
events.summary: Romanian director Cristian Mungiu has won the Palme d’Or for 'Fjord', a tense family drama starring Sebastian Stan and Renate Reinsve. In FRANCE 24’s special Cannes awards programme, Eve Jackson and critic Emma Jones break down all the major winners from the closing ceremony – from the Grand Prix for 'Minotaur' to the double acting prizes for 'All of a Sudden' and 'Coward'.

events.title: Ebola epidemic: Ten African countries at risk
events.summary: Africa CDC has issued a fresh warning this Saturday, saying up to ten additional African countries are now at risk as the Ebola outbreak spreads across the region. Those countries are Angola, Burundi, the Central African Republic, the Republic of Congo, Ethiopia, Kenya, Rwanda, South Sudan, Tanzania and Zambia. Uganda has confirmed three new cases, while the Red Cross says three volunteers have died in eastern DR Congo after contracting the virus in March. In Ituri’s Mongbwalu, residents attacked and burned part of an Ebola treatment centre, forcing 18 suspected cases to flee, while another centre in Rwampara was also set on fire.

events.title: 'Fjord' by Romania's Cristian Mungiu wins Palme d'Or at Cannes Film Festival
Fjord wins Palme d’Or at Cannes Film Festival
events.summary:
🎞️The film "Fjord" directed by Cristian Mungiu, starring Renate Reinsve and Sebastian Stan, wins the top prize at the 79th Cannes Film Festival.
 
Inspired by real cases, the film follows a Romanian family who move to Norway and face accusations of child abuse amid a profound cultural clash.

events.title: Film stars take to the Cannes red carpet ahead of Palme d'Or announcement
events.summary: FRANCE 24 film critic Emma Jones reports from the red carpet at the 79th Cannes Film Festival in southern France.

events.title: 'Significant movement here in Washington': US and Iran report progress towards deal on ending war
events.summary: FRANCE 24 correspondent Fraser Jackson speaks from Washington, DC on the reported progress in the talks on ending the US-Israeli war on Iran.

events.title: AI, Palantir & the Pushback: AI, Palantir & the Pushback: The London Mayor Vs the Met Police
events.summary:
As concerns continue to grow over artificial intelligence, national security and digital sovereignty, London Mayor Sadiq Khan has blocked a proposed fifty-million-pound contract between the Metropolitan Police and AI giant Palantir. The company had been expected to provide advanced tools to help police process intelligence in criminal investigations.
 
To discuss the decision, FRANCE 24’s Gavin Lee is joined by Susan Hall, leader of the Conservative Party in the London Assembly.

events.title: 'Fjord' by Romania's Cristian Mungiu wins Palme d'Or at Cannes Film Festival
events.summary: The Norway-set drama "Fjord" by Romanian director Cristian Mungiu was awarded the Palme d'Or at the closing ceremony of the 79th Cannes Film Festival as critics hailed a strong field of contenders directed by auteurs from Russia, Iran, Japan and beyond.

events.title: Israeli minister Ben-Gvir banned from entering France
events.summary: Andrew Hiliar has more.

events.title: 🔴 US and Iran are ‘getting a lot closer’ to finalising agreement, Trump says
Women's Champions League final: Lyon, Barcelona clash for European crown
events.summary: Selena Sykes has more.

events.title: Ebola epidemic threatens ten African countries in addition to DRC and Uganda
events.summary: Ten African countries are at risk of being affected by the Ebola virus, in addition to the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda, the African Union's health agency warned on Saturday.

events.title: Senegal's president Faye sacks PM Sonko after months of tension
events.summary: Paul Melly, Consulting Fellow, Africa Programme, Chatham House, is our guest.

events.title: Uganda health authorities confirms new Ebola cases, linked to DR Congo
events.summary: Three new Ebola cases have been confirmed in Uganda, health authorities said Saturday, after the World Health Organization raised the risk from the deadly outbreak to the highest level for neighbouring Democratic Republic of Congo.

events.title: Senegal's president fires prime minister after months of tensions
events.summary: Caitlin Kelly, our corespondent in Dakar, has more.

events.title: Coal mine explosion in China kills 90 people, state media say
events.summary: A gas explosion at a coal mine in China’s northern Shanxi province killed at least 90 people, state media said on Saturday, in the country’s deadliest mining accident in recent years.

events.title: Ebola: WHO raises health risk to 'very high' in DR Congo and new cases in Uganda
events.summary: Gabriela Arenas, Coordinator for Regional Operations in Africa IFRC in Kenya, is our guest.

events.title: Russia says 16 killed in strike on student dorm that it blamed on Ukraine
events.summary: The death toll from a drone strike ​on a student dorm in the Russian-controlled Luhansk region of eastern Ukraine has risen to 16, with most of the victims young women, Russian officials said on Saturday, after a heated U.N. debate on the incident.

events.title: 'At least 24' people killed after suicide bomb targets train carrying military personnel
events.summary: At least 24 people have died after a suicide bomber targeted a train carrying military personnel in Pakistan's southwestern province of Balochistan, officials have said.

events.title: At least four killed in Kyiv airstrikes as Zelenskyy claims hypersonic missile used during attacks
events.summary: At least four people have been killed in in Ukraine after a wave of Russian missile and drone strikes, regional officials have said, as Volodymyr Zelenskyy reported that Moscow had used a hypersonic missile to target Kyiv.

events.title: Riot police clash with protesters in Serbia after anti-government rally
events.summary: Clashes broke out in Belgrade after tens of thousands of people protested against Serbia's government.

events.title: Gun suspect killed after opening fire at White House security checkpoint
events.summary: The suspect in a shooting near the White House has died after being shot by police officers, according to the US Secret Service (USSS).

events.title: British man killed in action while fighting alongside Ukrainian troops
events.summary: A British man has been killed in action in Ukraine, the government has confirmed.

events.title: France bans Israeli minister over 'unspeakable' video
events.summary: France has banned Israel’s national security minister, Itamar Ben-Gvir, after he shared video of himself taunting activists from a flotilla to Gaza.

events.title: Formula 1 legend 'injured during home invasion'
events.summary: Police in Switzerland are investigating a home invasion in which four-time Formula 1 world champion Alain Prost was reportedly injured.

events.title: 90 killed and nine trapped following gas explosion at coal mine in China
events.summary: At least 90 people have been killed following a gas explosion at a coal mine in northern China, state media reports.

events.title: Hezbollah MP reveals three things needed for lasting ceasefire with Israel
events.summary: Despite ongoing negotiations, the US-brokered, month-old ceasefire between Lebanon and Israel has failed to halt fighting between the Israel Defence Forces (IDF) and Hezbollah, the Iran-backed Shia Muslim armed group.

events.title: Three new Ebola cases declared in Uganda - as DR Congo's World Cup team told to isolate
events.summary: Three new cases of Ebola have been declared in Uganda, bringing the total number of confirmed cases in the current outbreak there to five.

events.title: Powerful bomb explodes near railway track in Pakistan, killing at least 19 people
events.summary: A suicide bomber detonated an explosives-laden vehicle near a railway track as a passenger train passed through the southwestern Pakistani city of Quetta, killing at least 19 people and wounding over 70 others, officials said

events.title: Russia pounds Kyiv with missiles and drones, shaking city center and injuring 10
events.summary: Russia has launched an intense missile and drone attack on Kyiv, shaking buildings across the city center

events.title: 9-story building under construction in the Philippines collapses; 21 missing
events.summary: A nine-story building under construction in a city north of the Philippine capital Manila

events.title: Britain's navy prepares to clear mines in the Strait of Hormuz while waiting for a peace deal
events.summary: The U.K.’s Royal Navy is preparing for a potential mine-clearing operation in the Strait of Hormuz

events.title: Venezuela's Machado vows another run for presidency, eyes return from exile
events.summary: Nobel Peace Prize laureate María Corina Machado is planning to run for the job of Venezuela's president again

events.title: Peru's Catholic Church holds a symbolic ceremony in apology for Indigenous land dispossession
events.summary: The highest Catholic authorities in Peru have held a symbolic reparation ceremony for an Indigenous community affected by land dispossession

events.title: Moroccan king pardons Senegalese fans jailed over chaotic Africa Cup final
events.summary: Morocco’s king has granted an official pardon to Senegalese soccer fans jailed over hooliganism during the Africa Cup of Nations final

events.title: Iran and US are close to an understanding aimed at ending the war, officials say
events.summary: The United States and Iran are close to agreeing on a memorandum of understanding to end the war, according to two regional officials and a diplomat

events.title: Two traffic accidents in Cambodia kill 14 garment factory workers and injure 93 others
events.summary: Two separate traffic accidents in Cambodia have killed at least 14 garment factory workers and injured 93 others, mostly women

events.title: Here are some of China's major coal mining disasters this century
events.summary: A gas explosion at a coal mine in Shanxi, China, has killed at least 90 people, according to state media

events.title: Ukrainian drone attack triggers fire at a Russian oil terminal

events.summary: Some Cubans who’ve been able to buy solar panels say it’s a game changer, but most rely on other alternatives including charcoal, despite health concerns and the steep cost.

events.title: Graphic: Track U.S., state and county gas prices
events.summary: Are gas prices declining? NBC News is tracking national, state and county prices at the pump.

events.title: Map: See how gas prices have changed in the county where you live
events.summary: How much gas prices have increased during the Iran war depends entirely on where you live, an NBC News analysis of average gas price data from AAA shows.

events.title: House Republicans cancel vote curbing Trump on Iran
events.summary: House Republicans delayed a vote on a war powers resolution that would restrict President Donald Trump’s ability to continue U.S. military operations in Iran.

events.title: Iran-U.S. diplomacy intensifies as Trump seeks ‘right answers,’ Tehran signals gaps ‘reduced’
events.summary: A new burst of diplomatic action intensified Thursday in a push to break the deadlock between the United States and Iran.

events.title: Trump speaks with Israel’s prime minister on potential Iran agreement
events.summary: President Trump posted on his social media platform on Saturday that an agreement to end the war with Iran has been “largely negotiated,” adding that he also spoke with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu. NBC News’ Keir Simmons reports.

events.title: Greenlanders protest against Trump as the U.S. opens new consulate
events.summary: Hundreds gathered at the new U.S. consulate in Greenland to protest President Trump’s ambition for greater influence in the Danish territory. In January, the White House said Trump was considering how to acquire Greenland, saying it was strategically vital for security in the Arctic.

events.title: Ebola treatment center set alight in Congo
events.summary: An Ebola treatment center in the Democratic Republic of Congo was set alight as a group tried to retrieve the body of a person who died of the virus. The treatment center was not operational but was used to store bodies before a proper burial, an important step in containing Ebola outbreaks.

events.title: Gaza flotilla activists allege abuse in Israeli detention
events.summary: Activists from the Global Sumud Flotilla, whose boats were intercepted in international waters by Israeli forces, claim they were beaten and mistreated while detained.

events.title: Absence of USAID likely slowed Ebola detection and response, former officials say
events.summary: The Trump administration hollowed out USAID last year, but the State Department denied the changes have hampered its response.

events.title: How the Middle East conflict is worsening Somalia’s slide into famine
events.summary: Mustafe Denbil runs an agricultural shop and farm in northern Somalia, but three months after he ordered fertilizer and pesticides, the supplies his business needs are still stranded in Dubai

events.title: 3 Red Cross volunteers die of Ebola as number of cases rise in Uganda
events.summary: Three Red Cross volunteers have died, believed to have contracted the Ebola virus during a humanitarian mission in the Democratic Republic of the Congo in March, the organization said.“At the time of the intervention, the community was not aware of the Ebola Virus Disease outbreak, and the outbreak had not yet been identified.

events.title: Russia blames Ukraine, vows retaliation as 16 killed in strike on student dorm
events.summary: The death toll from a drone strike on a student dorm in the Russian-controlled Luhansk region of eastern Ukraine has risen to 16, with most of the victims young women, Russian officials said on Saturday, after a heated ⁠U.N.

events.title: Trump’s envoy went to Greenland to make ‘friends.’ They were left unimpressed.
events.summary: President Donald Trump’s envoy to Greenland says he got a warm welcome on his first visit this week.

events.title: India’s ‘Cockroach Janata Party’ began as a joke. Then millions joined in.
events.summary: What began as an internet punchline is turning into something more serious

events.title: Misinfo, strained resources and armed conflict hamper Ebola response
events.summary: One of the first cases of Ebola that Rose Tchwenko heard about in the current outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo was in a healthcare worker.

events.title: Police urge witnesses to come forward in former Prince Andrew investigation
events.summary: British police urged witnesses to come forward on Friday as they continued to investigate potential offenses by former Prince Andrew related to Jeffrey Epstein.

events.title: Mamdani announces $50 World Cup ticket lottery for New York City residents
events.summary: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani said Thursday that the city will use a lottery system to distribute 1,000 World Cup tickets at $50 apiece to residents of the five boroughs

events.title: Gaza flotilla activists deported from Israel as outrage over treatment grows
events.summary: Gaza flotilla activists who were detained by Israel and later pinned to the ground to the taunts of Israel’s far-right national security minister have been released from prison and will be deported to Turkey on Thursday, officials said.

events.title: Trump says he will hold protocol-shattering call with Taiwan leader
events.summary: Taiwan President Lai Ching-te would be “happy” to speak with President Donald Trump, the Beijing-claimed island’s foreign ministry said Thursday, after Trump suggested the two leaders might talk in what would be a major break in U.S. presidential protocol that is likely to antagonize China.

events.title: Raúl Castro indictment brings back history of Cuba’s downing of U.S. civilian planes
events.summary: Cuba shot down two Brothers to the Rescue U.S. civilian planes in 1996, killing four Cuban Americans and drawing swift condemnation from the U.S. and the international community.

events.title: Ebola death toll rises as two Americans are flown to Europe for monitoring
events.summary: The suspected death toll from the Ebola outbreak in central Africa rose again Wednesday as a group of potentially exposed Americans was arriving in Europe for monitoring and the World Health Organization warned a vaccine was still months away.

events.title: Putin and Xi say ties are at ‘unprecedented’ high at friendly summit after Trump visit
events.summary: China-Russia relations are at a “historic high,” Chinese leader Xi Jinping said as he met with Russian President Vladimir Putin in Beijing days after hosting U.S. President Donald Trump.

events.title: Could a soccer match help mend ties between North and South Korea?
events.summary: A North Korean women’s soccer team took the field Wednesday in South Korea, a rare cross-border clash as Kim Jong Un pursues a more hostile policy toward his neighbor.
